name: MIDI In CHOP
description: The MIDI In CHOP reads Note events, Controller events, Program Change events, System Exclusive messages and Timing events from both MIDI devices and files. See also MIDI In Map CHOP.
summary: The MIDI In CHOP reads Note events, Controller events, Program Change events, System Exclusive messages and Timing events from both MIDI devices and files. See also MIDI In Map CHOP.

parameters.name: Active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: Enable or disable the MIDI In .

parameters.name: MIDI Source
parameters.label: MIDI Source
parameters.description:
source -  - Get MIDI input from a device or a file.

 device - The MIDI events come live from a device. file - The MIDI events come from an existing file.

parameters.name: Device
parameters.label: Device
parameters.description: device - The MIDI events come live from a device. file - The MIDI events come from an existing file.

parameters.name: Device Table
parameters.label: Device Table
parameters.description: device -  to the MIDI device Table DAT

parameters.name: Device ID
parameters.label: Device ID
parameters.description: id - Specify the id of which device to use.

parameters.name: MIDI File
parameters.label: MIDI File
parameters.description: file - If MIDI file is selected as the MIDI source use this field to specify the name of the MIDI file to read. The file can be read in from disk or from the web. Use http:// when specifying a URL.

parameters.name: Read Entire MIDI File
parameters.label: Read Entire MIDI File
parameters.description: entire - If enabled, the entire MIDI file is read. Otherwise, the Start and End parameters on the Channel page determine the segment of the file to read.

parameters.name: Reset	Channels
parameters.label: Reset	Channels
parameters.description: resetchannels - Deletes all channels when set to On, new channels will not be added until Reset Channels is turned Off.

parameters.name: Reset Channels Pulse
parameters.label: Reset Channels Pulse
parameters.description: resetchannelspulse - Instantly deletes all channels.

parameters.name: Reset	Values
parameters.label: Reset	Values
parameters.description: resetvalues - Resets all channel values to 0 when On, channel values will not be updated until Reset Values is turned Off.

parameters.name: Simplified Output
parameters.label: Simplified Output
parameters.description: simplified - When this is on channels are automatically created when MIDI signal is detected from the selected MIDI device.

parameters.name: Preserve Pulses
parameters.label: Preserve Pulses
parameters.description: preservepulses - When on, quick value transitions (pulses) are spaced out over consecutive output samples. Use this option when pulse frequencies approach or exceed the timeline rate, otherwise they risk overlapping each other and being lost.

parameters.name: 1 Based Index
parameters.label: 1 Based Index
parameters.description: onebased - Make the index 1 based instead of the default 0 based.

parameters.name: MIDI Channels
parameters.label: MIDI Channels
parameters.description:
channel - The  may read from any number of MIDI channels, numbered 1-16. Ranges and multiple entries are supported (i.e. "1 4 6", "1-7 12", "1-5:2").	
If Channel Prefix is left blank, then the input streams from multiple MIDI channels will be combined into one set of  channels (i.e., a "note 64 on" event on channel 12 followed by a "note 64 off" event on channel 8 appears in the note 64  channel as a single note).

parameters.name: Channel Prefix
parameters.label: Channel Prefix
parameters.description: prefix - When recording from multiple MIDI channels, putting a string like "ch" in this parameter causes the MIDI channel to be split into separate  channels per MIDI channel. Otherwise the MIDI channels are all merged into one set of  channels.

parameters.name: Record Method
parameters.label: Record Method
parameters.description:
recordtype -  - Determine what to record.

 single - This only outputs the current value of notes, controllers, etc. It does not retain prior values. It creates a  at frame 1 always, so the  causes cooking only when MIDI events come in. current - Same as "Single ", but the  frame is always at the current frame, so the  changes every frame, causing other CHOPs to recook every frame. ind - The  frame is always at the current Time Slice. timeline - The entire length of the timeline is recorded.

parameters.name: Single Frame
parameters.label: Single Frame
parameters.description: single - This only outputs the current value of notes, controllers, etc. It does not retain prior values. It creates a  at frame 1 always, so the  causes cooking only when MIDI events come in. current - Same as "Single ", but the  frame is always at the current frame, so the  changes every frame, causing other CHOPs to recook every frame. ind - The  frame is always at the current Time Slice. timeline - The entire length of the timeline is recorded.

parameters.name: Record
parameters.label: Record
parameters.description: record - This parameter is used as a button to start and stop recording into the  channels.

parameters.name: Note Name
parameters.label: Note Name
parameters.description: notename - Put an "n" in here to generate channels for note events. It is the base name of the  channel used to record notes. If blank, notes are ignored. If the Note Output parameter is set to Separate Channels, then  channels will be created for each note in the Note , of the form: notename

parameters.name: Note Scope
parameters.label: Note Scope
parameters.description: notescope - The scope of notes to record. Multiple ranges and notes can be recorded (i.e., "50-60", "64 65 66 70-80").

parameters.name: Note Output
parameters.label: Note Output
parameters.description:
notemeth -  - Describes how multiple notes are handled. (As one channel, or individual).

 mult - One  channel per MIDI channel is used for all notes. The value in the note channel is the number of the note currently playing (in the case of multiple notes playing, the most recent note is selected). sep - Each note number gets its own  channel.

parameters.name: One Multiplexed Channel
parameters.label: One Multiplexed Channel
parameters.description: mult - One  channel per MIDI channel is used for all notes. The value in the note channel is the number of the note currently playing (in the case of multiple notes playing, the most recent note is selected). sep - Each note number gets its own  channel.

parameters.name: Velocity
parameters.label: Velocity
parameters.description:
velocity -  - Describes how multiple velocity events are recorded. (Separate channels or combined in one channel as separate samples).

 veloff - Velocity is not recorded. velencode - The velocity is recorded as the amplitude of the note. Only valid when Note Output is set to Separate Channels. velsep - Velocity is recorded in separate  channels. Each note channel will have a corresponding velocity channel.

parameters.name: Off
parameters.label: Off
parameters.description: veloff - Velocity is not recorded. velencode - The velocity is recorded as the amplitude of the note. Only valid when Note Output is set to Separate Channels. velsep - Velocity is recorded in separate  channels. Each note channel will have a corresponding velocity channel.

parameters.name: Velocity Name
parameters.label: Velocity Name
parameters.description: velname - When Velocity is set to Separate Channels, this parameter is the base name of the Velocity  channel (try "v"). If blank, no velocity channels will be recorded.

parameters.name: Aftertouch Name
parameters.label: Aftertouch Name
parameters.description: aftername - The base name of the polyphonic Aftertouch  channels. One aftertouch channel is created for each note in the Note . If blank, no aftertouch channels will be created.

parameters.name: Pressure Name
parameters.label: Pressure Name
parameters.description: pressname - The name of the Channel Pressure channel. If multiple channels are being recorded, all channel pressure changes of interest will be recording on this  channel. If blank, this channel will not be created.

parameters.name: Normalize
parameters.label: Normalize
parameters.description:
notenorm -  - Note values can be normalized for convenience:

 off - When Normalize is Off, 7 bit controllers will return values in the range 0-127 and 14 bit controllers will return values in the range 0-16383. 0to1 - These values are normalized to lie in the range 0 to 1.

parameters.name: None
parameters.label: None
parameters.description: off - When Normalize is Off, 7 bit controllers will return values in the range 0-127 and 14 bit controllers will return values in the range 0-16383. 0to1 - These values are normalized to lie in the range 0 to 1.

parameters.name: Pitch Wheel Name
parameters.label: Pitch Wheel Name
parameters.description: pitchname - The name of the Pitch Wheel  channel. Pitch wheel values range from -1 to +1. If blank, this channel will not be created. Put "p" in here to generate a channel.

parameters.name: Controller Name
parameters.label: Controller Name
parameters.description: controlname - The base name of the Control Change  channels. The channel names are appended with the controller index (0-127). If blank, control changes will not be recorded. It typically contains "c".

parameters.name: Controller Type
parameters.label: Controller Type
parameters.description:
controltype -  - There are 128 different controllers available. By choosing By Index Only, you can specify any number of controllers in the Control Index parameter. Otherwise, you can select a controller from the list from this menu. Some controllers have multiple instances, such as Sound Controllers 1-10. Selecting a controller with multiple instances allows you to use the Control Index parameter to select which instances you want. Any invalid control indices will be ignored.

 index - Specify the controller by its index, as opposed to specifying a specific type below. n0 - n1 - n2 - n3 - n4 - n5 - n6 - n7 - n8 - n9 - n10 - n11 - n12 - n13 - n14 - n15 - n16 - n17 - n18 - n19 - n20 - n21 - n22 - n23 -

parameters.name: By Index Only
parameters.label: By Index Only
parameters.description: index - Specify the controller by its index, as opposed to specifying a specific type below. n0 - n1 - n2 - n3 - n4 - n5 - n6 - n7 - n8 - n9 - n10 - n11 - n12 - n13 - n14 - n15 - n16 - n17 - n18 - n19 - n20 - n21 - n22 - n23 -

parameters.name: Controller Index
parameters.label: Controller Index
parameters.description: controlind - Used to select controllers by number, or multiple controllers by ranges. When in By Index Only Controller Type mode, you can select up to the full 128 controllers or sub-ranges thereof (i.e. "1-10", "2 34 70", "1-32 70-80:2").

parameters.name: Controller Format
parameters.label: Controller Format
parameters.description:
format -  - Some controllers can be paired together to form 14 bit controllers, rather than the normal 7 bit controllers (controller indices 0-31, 98 and 100). Selecting 14 bit Controllers interprets a pair as one 14 bit controller. Otherwise, they are interpreted as separate 7 bit Controllers.

 7bit - Controllers are assumed to be regular 7 bit value messages. 14bit - Controllers are received as two 7 bit value messages.

parameters.name: 7 bit Controllers
parameters.label: 7 bit Controllers
parameters.description: 7bit - Controllers are assumed to be regular 7 bit value messages. 14bit - Controllers are received as two 7 bit value messages.

parameters.name: Unwrap
parameters.label: Unwrap
parameters.description: unwrap - When on, values do not jump between min and max, but are modified to be continuous ramps. Use with knob controllers for examples.

parameters.name: Program Change
parameters.label: Program Change
parameters.description: progname - The name of the Program Change  channel. All program change messages will be recorded onto this channel. If blank, this channel will not be created.

parameters.name: Timer Pulse Name
parameters.label: Timer Pulse Name
parameters.description: pulsename - Record timer pulse messages.

parameters.name: Timer Ramp Name
parameters.label: Timer Ramp Name
parameters.description: rampname - Record timer ramp messages.

parameters.name: Timer Period
parameters.label: Timer Period
parameters.description: timerperiod - Record timer period messages.

parameters.name: Timer Start
parameters.label: Timer Start
parameters.description: timerstart - Record timer start messages.

parameters.name: Ticks per Beat
parameters.label: Ticks per Beat
parameters.description: ticks - Specify the expected ticks per beat. This will influence the timer and bar output values.

parameters.name: Bar Ramp Name
parameters.label: Bar Ramp Name
parameters.description: barname - Output the current bar position.

parameters.name: Bar Period
parameters.label: Bar Period
parameters.description: barperiod - Output the current bar period events.

parameters.name: Bar Start
parameters.label: Bar Start
parameters.description: barstart - Output when a bar starts.

parameters.name: Bar Message
parameters.label: Bar Message
parameters.description: barmsg - Capture bar messages. Place a V in the message to specify which value the channel should have.

parameters.name: Song Pos Name
parameters.label: Song Pos Name
parameters.description: songpos - Capture song position messages.

parameters.name: System Exclusive
parameters.label: System Exclusive
parameters.description:
sysex - Sequence of system exclusive message handlers



  sysex0name - Name of a specific exclusive message to be captured.



  sysex0msg - Contents of the exclusive message. Place a V in the message to specify which value the channel should have.

parameters.name: Channel Name
parameters.label: Channel Name
parameters.description: sysex0name - Name of a specific exclusive message to be captured.

parameters.name: Message
parameters.label: Message
parameters.description: sysex0msg - Contents of the exclusive message. Place a V in the message to specify which value the channel should have.

parameters.name: Start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description:
start - Defines where recording begins. In "Tie to Time Line" mode, any events received before the start time will be ignored. In "Time Line Independent" mode, recording will start at this point and continue on (not looping back). If reading from a MIDI File, Start/End will determine the start of the segment to read. 
Start and end of the interval, expressed in Units (seconds, frames or samples). The parameters are expressed in the Units found on the Common page. To set the  to be 100 samples long, Set Units to Samples, Single  Off and Start / End to 0 and 99.

parameters.name: Start Unit
parameters.label: Start Unit
parameters.description:
startunit -  - Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

 samples - Use samples. frames - Use frames. seconds - Use seconds.

parameters.name: I
parameters.label: I
parameters.description: samples - Use samples. frames - Use frames. seconds - Use seconds.

parameters.name: End
parameters.label: End
parameters.description: end - Defines the end of the segment to read for MIDI Files.

parameters.name: End Unit
parameters.label: End Unit
parameters.description:
endunit -  - Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

 samples - Use samples. frames - Use frames. seconds - Use seconds.

parameters.name: Sample Rate
parameters.label: Sample Rate
parameters.description:
rate - Defines the sample rate of this , in samples per second. If the sample rate is too low, a rapidly changing input may be misrepresented.	
Note: If the sample rate is too low, you may miss MIDI events. A note event may set a sample value to 1, and then the next note event less than 1/30 second later can set it to 0 on the same sample in the  channel. So the event will be missed. Make the sample rate higher, like 600, to catch these events, or make sure that your on-off events coming in are a minimum time separation, as can be achieved through Opcode's MAX.

parameters.name: Extend Left
parameters.label: Extend Left
parameters.description:
left -  - The left extend conditions (before range).

 hold - Hold the current value of the channel. slope - Continue the slope before the start of the channel. cycle - Cycle the channel repeatedly. mirror - Cycle the channel repeatedly, mirroring every other cycle. default - Use the constant value specified in the Default Value parameter.

parameters.name: Hold
parameters.label: Hold
parameters.description: hold - Hold the current value of the channel. slope - Continue the slope before the start of the channel. cycle - Cycle the channel repeatedly. mirror - Cycle the channel repeatedly, mirroring every other cycle. default - Use the constant value specified in the Default Value parameter.

parameters.name: Extend Right
parameters.label: Extend Right
parameters.description:
right -  - The right extend conditions (after range).

 hold - Hold the current value of the channel. slope - Continue the slope after the end of the channel. cycle - Cycle the channel repeatedly. mirror - Cycle the channel repeatedly, mirroring every other cycle. default - Use the constant value specified in the Default Value parameter.

parameters.name: Default Value
parameters.label: Default Value
parameters.description: defval - The value used for the Default Value extend condition.

parameters.name: Time Slice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: timeslice - Turning this on forces the channels to be "Time Sliced".  A  is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: Scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: scope - To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a  string on the Common page.

parameters.name: Sample Rate Match
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description:
srselect -  - Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Options are not available.

 first - Use rate of first input to resample others. max - Resample to the highest sample rate. min - Resample to the lowest sample rate. err - Doesn't accept conflicting sample rates.

parameters.name: Resample At First Input's Rate
parameters.label: Resample At First Input's Rate
parameters.description: first - Use rate of first input to resample others. max - Resample to the highest sample rate. min - Resample to the lowest sample rate. err - Doesn't accept conflicting sample rates.

parameters.name: Export Method
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description:
exportmethod -  - This will determine how to connect the  channel to the parameter. Refer to the Export article for more information.

 datindex - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the index of the channel in the . datname - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the name of the channel in the . autoname - The channel is the full destination of where to export to, such has geo1/transform1:tx.

parameters.name: DAT Table by Index
parameters.label: DAT Table by Index
parameters.description: datindex - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the index of the channel in the . datname - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the name of the channel in the . autoname - The channel is the full destination of where to export to, such has geo1/transform1:tx.

parameters.name: Export Root
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: autoexportroot - This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by Channel Name is : are relative to.

parameters.name: Export Table
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: exporttable - The  used to hold the export information when using the  Table  Methods (See above).
